MINISTRY OF LAW, JUSTICE AND COMPANY AFFAIRS (Legislative Department) New Delhi, the cOth August, 1982/Bh.adrct 8, 1904 (Saka) The following Act of Parliament received the assent of the President •on the 28th August, 1982, and is hereby published for general informa- tion:— THE GOVERNORS ( E M O L U M E ^ L&, A L L O ' V / A ^ O E O AIN'D PRIVILEGES) Act, i982 No. 43 or 1982 [28th, August, 1932] An Act. to determine the emoluments, allowances and privileges of Governors. B E it enacted by Parliament in the Thirty-third Year of the Republic of India as follows: — 1. (1) This Act may be called the Governors (Emoluments, Allow- ances and Privileges) Act, 1982. : extent (2) It extends to the whole of India except t h e State of J a m m u and " e n ^ K a s h m i r . * merit. (3) 'It shall come into force on such date as the Central Government . m a y . by notification in the Official Gazette, appoint. 2. Subject to any rules made in this behalf, a Governor and t h e mem- Medical bers of his . family shall be entitled during t h e t e r m of his office and t r e a t " thereafter also, to free medical attendance, accommodation and treat- m e n t ment in the hospitals maintained by the Central Government or -the Government of any State. 8. (2) A Governor shall be entitled to use without payment of rent Convey- or hire, such n u m b e r of motor vehicles as the President may by order ance. - determine. (2) No charge shall fall on a Governor personally in respect of the maintenance of t h e motor vehicles referred to in sub-section (1). (3) The use of t h e motor vehicles referred to in sub-section (1) by the members of the family of a Governor shall be regulated by rules made in this behalf. 9. Subject to any rules made in this behalf, a Governor shall be entitled to travelling allowance for himself and the members of his family and for the transport of his. and his family's effects— (a) in respect of the journey for assuming office from the place where h e is ordinarily residing to the place of his duty; and (b) in respect of the journey on relinquishing office from t h e place of his d u t y to t h e place where he would ordinarily reside thereafter or if he is to take -up any other office under the Govern- ment (including t h e office of the Governor of another State) after such relinquishment, to the place of duty with respect to such other office. , 10. Subject to any rules made in this behalf, a Governor shall be entitled to such allowances for renewing the furnishings and for the maintenance of t h e official residences, as the President may by order determine. ^ Travel- ling allow- ance on ass^ump- tjon or. vacation of office. 11. For the purpose of enabling a Governor to discharge conveniently and with dignity the duties of his office, he shall be— (i) entitled to such other privileges as may be prescribed by rules made in this behalf, and (ii) paid, subject to any rules made in this behalf, such amount, as the President may, by genera] or special order, determine by way of the following, namely: — (a) entertainment allowance; (b) hospitality grant; (c) household establishment expenses; (d) office expenses; (e) contract allowance, i.e., an allowance for miscellaneous expenses; (f) tour expenses; and • (g) such ether allowances or expenses as may be provided IOT by rules. Allow- ances for renew- ing furni- shings and for mainten- ance of official resi- dences. Other privileges and/ allow- ances. -- 3 of 5 -- THE GAZETTE OF INDIA EXTRAORDINARY [FART I I - Addi- tional expenses. Fawqp to make rules, 12 (1) Where, in the case of any Governor, the President is satisfied that ihe amount authorised under this Act by way of any allowances or for meeting any expenses with respect to any matter reqmres to be in- creased or that a need has arisen to sanction expenses with respect to a,y matter for which provision, though permissible has not been made n the rules made under this Act, he may by special order m c r e a e s u h amount or sanction such expenses to such extent as may be specified m such order. (2) An order may be made under sub-section (1) so as to have retrospective effect. (3) Every order made under this section shall be laid, as soon as may be after it is made, before both Houses of Parliament. 13 (1) The President may, by notification in the Official Gazette, make rules for the purpose of giving effect to the provisions of this Act. (2) In particular, and without prejudice to the generality of the fore- going p w e r , such rules may provide for all or any of the f o l l o w ^ - matters, namely: — (a) grant'of leave to a Governor under section 4; (b) matters relating to the household establishment provided to a Governor under section 6; M medical attendance, accommodation and treatment of a Governor and the members of his family under section ,; (d) the use of motor vehicles by the members of the family of a Governor under sub-section (3) of section 8; (e) the travelling allowance on assumption or vacation of office of a Governor under section 9; (f) allowances for renewing the furnishings and for the main- tenance of the official residences under section 10, . (a) the privileges to which a Governor is entitled and the allowance" o? expenses payable to a Governor under section 11. (3) Every rule made by the President under this Act shall be laid a s soon as may .be after it is made, before each House of Parliament, whfle it is in session, for a total period of thirty days, which may be romnrised in one session or in two or more successive sessions, and if, S e the expiry of the session immediately following the session or the successive' sessions aforesaid, both Houses agree in making any L ^ t f n n in the rule or both Houses agree that the rule should not be ^ a d e t h r u l e t a l l t h e r e a f t e r have effect only in such modified form T be'of no effect as the case may be; so, however, that any sucn modi- S a ^ o n or annulment shall be without prejudice to the validity of any- ihing praviously done under that rule. -- 4 of 5 -- SEC. 1] THE GAZETTE OF INDIA EXTRAORDINARY 5 ^ I ^____ '^ 14. (I) Every special order issued, before the commencement of this Valida Act, by the President in regard to the allowances, expenses (including tion- medical expenses) or privileges of any .Governor (other than the Gov- ernor of Nagaland) shall, notwithstanding that such order was made with retrospective effect, or is inconsistent with any general order issued under any law with respect to those matters, be as valid and effective as if such special order formed part of this sub-section and this sub- section had been in force at all material times- (2) Every general or special order issued, before the commencement of this Act, by the President in regard to the allowances, expenses (in- cluding medical expenses) or privileges of the Governor of Nagaland shall, notwithstanding that such order was made with retrospective effect, be as valid and effective as if it formed part of this sub-section and this sub-section had been in force at all material times. . 15. Nothing contained in this Act or the roles made thereunder shall have effect so as to diminish the emoluments and allowances of any Saving Governor during his term of office. R. V. S. PERI SASTRI, Secy, to the Govt, of India. -- 5 of 5 --
